topic: In China employee termination: myth-vs-fact legal clarity guide for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination myths foreign employers
related_keywords: foreign employer China labor dispute exposure

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors fact-checking for common foreign-employer misconceptions on termination routes and consequences.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports practical interpretation when myths stem from oversimplified policy assumptions.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Used to ground dispute-facing facts and evidence-related myth corrections.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Supports fact checks linked to termination-month payroll and contribution closure responsibilities.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Best-fit practical source for foreign-reader myth correction and implementation caution.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from position-paper format to myth-vs-fact format; retained core legal source hierarchy for factual stability.
Excluded low-fit generalized commentary and tax-first materials to keep legal myth correction precise.
Re-centered China Briefing as the practical layer supporting foreign-reader comprehension, not as primary legal authority.
consensus: Foreign employers commonly overestimate managerial discretion in China termination cases.
Legal route classification and evidence coherence drive dispute outcomes more than procedural speed.
A myth-vs-fact format improves comprehension for non-local readers when each correction ties to source hierarchy.
uncertainties: City-level enforcement style and document expectations vary.
Complex payment treatment details can differ by fact pattern and local practice.
Official English texts may not fully reflect evolving Chinese-language implementation practice.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for myth-vs-fact because sources support high-confidence factual correction while preserving practical readability for foreign audiences.
